data.content:
### Build Sandbox Template with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.7.0/template/page.mdx

Builds a sandbox template using a Dockerfile. It can automatically detect Dockerfiles or be pointed to a specific one. Options allow customization of the build path, template name, start command, readiness command, team association, configuration file, CPU/memory resources, build arguments, and cache behavior.

```bash
e2b template build [options] [template]

Options:
 - -p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory 
 - -d, --dockerfile <file>: specify path to Dockerfile. By default E2B tries to find e2b.Dockerfile or Dockerfile in root directory. 
 - -n, --name <template-name>: specify sandbox template name. You can use the template name to start the sandbox with SDK. The template name must be lowercase and contain only letters, numbers, dashes and underscores. 
 - -c, --cmd <start-command>: specify command that will be executed when the sandbox is started. 
 - --ready-cmd <ready-command>: specify command that will need to exit 0 for the template to be ready. 
 - -t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team). 
 - --config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory. 
 - --cpu-count <cpu-count>: specify the number of CPUs that will be used to run the sandbox. The default value is 2. 
 - --memory-mb <memory-mb>: specify the amount of memory in megabytes that will be used to run the sandbox. Must be an even number. The default value is 512. 
 - --build-arg <args...>: specify additional build arguments for the build command. The format should be <varname>=<value>. 
 - --no-cache: skip cache when building the template.
```

--------------------------------

### POST /files/make_dir

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.11.1/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Create a new directory in the sandbox filesystem.

```APIDOC
## POST /files/make_dir

### Description
Create a new directory and all directories along the way if needed on the specified path.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
`/files/make_dir`

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **path** (str) - Required - Path to a new directory. For example '/dirA/dirB' when creating 'dirB'.
- **user** (Username) - Optional - Run the operation as this user (default: "user")
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **boolean** - `True` if the directory was created, `False` if the directory already exists.
```

--------------------------------

### Filesystem.makeDir()

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.2.3/filesystem/page.mdx

Create a new directory in the sandbox filesystem.

```APIDOC
## Filesystem.makeDir()

### Description
Create a new directory and all directories along the way if needed on the specified path.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
`/filesystem/mkdir`

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **path** (string) - Required - Path to a new directory. For example '/dirA/dirB' when creating 'dirB'.
- **opts** (FilesystemRequestOpts) - Optional - Connection options.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **boolean** - `true` if the directory was created, `false` if it already exists.

### Response Example
```json
{
  "created": true
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Build Sandbox Template (e2b CLI)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.2.0/template/page.mdx

Builds a sandbox template defined by a Dockerfile. It can specify the Dockerfile path, template name, start command, and resource allocation. Optionally, it can use build arguments and skip caching.

```bash
e2b template build [options] [template]

# Example with options:
e2b template build -n my-template -c "npm start" --cpu-count 4 --memory-mb 1024
```

--------------------------------

### Build Sandbox Template (CLI)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.5.2/template/page.mdx

Builds a sandbox template using a Dockerfile. It supports specifying the Dockerfile path, template name, start command, and build arguments. By default, it looks for e2b.Dockerfile or Dockerfile in the root directory.

```bash
e2b template build [options] [template]

Options:
 - `-p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory 
 - `-d, --dockerfile <file>: specify path to Dockerfile. By default E2B tries to find e2b.Dockerfile or Dockerfile in root directory. 
 - `-n, --name <template-name>: specify sandbox template name. You can use the template name to start the sandbox with SDK. The template name must be lowercase and contain only letters, numbers, dashes and underscores. 
 - `-c, --cmd <start-command>: specify command that will be executed when the sandbox is started. 
 - `--ready-cmd <ready-command>: specify command that will need to exit 0 for the template to be ready. 
 - `-t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team). 
 - `--config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory. 
 - `--cpu-count <cpu-count>: specify the number of CPUs that will be used to run the sandbox. The default value is 2. 
 - `--memory-mb <memory-mb>: specify the amount of memory in megabytes that will be used to run the sandbox. Must be an even number. The default value is 512. 
 - `--build-arg <args...>: specify additional build arguments for the build command. The format should be <varname>=<value>. 
 - `--no-cache: skip cache when building the template. 
```

--------------------------------

### POST /make_dir

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.3.1/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Creates a new directory at the specified path. Intermediate directories will be created if they do not exist.

```APIDOC
## POST /make_dir

### Description
Create a new directory and all directories along the way if needed on the specified path.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/make_dir

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **path** (string) - Required - Path to the new directory. For example '/dirA/dirB' when creating 'dirB'.
- **user** (Username) - Optional - The username to run the operation as. Defaults to 'user'.
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "path": "/new_parent_dir/new_subdir"
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **boolean** (bool) - `True` if the directory was created, `False` if the directory already exists.

#### Response Example
```json
true
```
```

--------------------------------

### Filesystem Make Directory API

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.0.1/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Create a new directory at the specified path, including any necessary parent directories.

```APIDOC
## POST /filesystem/make_dir

### Description
Create a new directory. If parent directories do not exist, they will be created as well.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/filesystem/make_dir

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **path** (str) - Required - The path for the new directory. For example, for '/dirA/dirB', 'dirB' will be created inside 'dirA'.
- **user** (Username) - Optional - Run the operation as this user. Defaults to "user".
- **request_timeout** (Optional[float]) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **created** (bool) - `True` if the directory was created, `False` if it already existed.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "created": true
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Build Sandbox Template - E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.4.3/template/page.mdx

Builds a sandbox template using a Dockerfile. It can specify the Dockerfile path, template name, start command, team association, CPU/memory limits, and build arguments. It also supports skipping the cache.

```bash
e2b template build [options] [template]

Options:
 - `-p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory 
 - `-d, --dockerfile <file>: specify path to Dockerfile. By default E2B tries to find e2b.Dockerfile or Dockerfile in root directory. 
 - `-n, --name <template-name>: specify sandbox template name. You can use the template name to start the sandbox with SDK. The template name must be lowercase and contain only letters, numbers, dashes and underscores. 
 - `-c, --cmd <start-command>: specify command that will be executed when the sandbox is started. 
 - `-t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team). 
 - `--config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory. 
 - `--cpu-count <cpu-count>: specify the number of CPUs that will be used to run the sandbox. The default value is 2. 
 - `--memory-mb <memory-mb>: specify the amount of memory in megabytes that will be used to run the sandbox. Must be an even number. The default value is 512. 
 - `--build-arg <args...>: specify additional build arguments for the build command. The format should be <varname>=<value>. 
 - `--no-cache: skip cache when building the template. 
```

--------------------------------

### Build Sandbox Template with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.8.0/template/page.mdx

Builds a sandbox template using a Dockerfile located in the root directory (e2b.Dockerfile or Dockerfile). This command also generates an e2b.toml configuration file. It supports specifying the build context path, Dockerfile path, template name, start command, readiness command, team ID, configuration file, CPU count, memory, build arguments, and cache behavior.

```bash
e2b template build [options] [template]

Options:
  -p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory
  -d, --dockerfile <file>: specify path to Dockerfile. By default E2B tries to find e2b.Dockerfile or Dockerfile in root directory.
  -n, --name <template-name>: specify sandbox template name. You can use the template name to start the sandbox with SDK. The template name must be lowercase and contain only letters, numbers, dashes and underscores.
  -c, --cmd <start-command>: specify command that will be executed when the sandbox is started.
  --ready-cmd <ready-command>: specify command that will need to exit 0 for the template to be ready.
  -t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team).
  --config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory.
  --cpu-count <cpu-count>: specify the number of CPUs that will be used to run the sandbox. The default value is 2.
  --memory-mb <memory-mb>: specify the amount of memory in megabytes that will be used to run the sandbox. Must be an even number. The default value is 512.
  --build-arg <args...>: specify additional build arguments for the build command. The format should be <varname>=<value>.
  --no-cache: skip cache when building the template.
```
